entities.name: Cyber AB
entities.notes: Accreditation body for the CMMC ecosystem; sets rules for C3PAOs and RPOs1. 103 authorized C3PAOs as of Nov 20268.

entities.name: DoD
entities.notes: Drives CMMC enforcement via 32 CFR (effective Dec 2024) and 48 CFR (effective Nov 2025) clauses now phasing into contracts.

entities.name: C3PAOs
entities.notes: 103 authorized C3PAOs8 serving ~100,000 DIB orgs; ~80,000 of those need Level 29. Only ~1% Level 2 certified to date8. The bottleneck. Independence rules: a C3PAO cannot consult on the engagement it assesses.

entities.name: DIBCAC
entities.notes: DCMA's industrial-base assessment arm. Conducted DIBCAC High pre-CMMC; remains relevant for Level 3 and ongoing DoD-direct audits.

entities.name: Vanta
entities.notes: Category leader. Strong SOC 2 muscle; CMMC coverage shipped 2024. Stated focus is SaaS multi-framework rather than DIB-native readiness.

entities.name: Drata
entities.notes: Close #2 to Vanta. Stronger FedRAMP story and growing CMMC coverage; SaaS-first by DNA.

entities.name: Secureframe
entities.notes: Strong AI/automation story. Partnered with RSM (C3PAO) for streamlined assessments7.

entities.name: Sprinto
entities.notes: Lower price point, India-heavy. CMMC not a stated focus.

entities.name: Thoropass
entities.notes: Formerly Laika. Bundles audit + automation. CMMC not a primary lane.

entities.name: DIB SMBs
entities.notes: 80,000+ small contractors expected to need Level 29, below the Tier-1 prime line. The unserved 0→1 segment Eagle Ridge targets.

entities.name: RPOs
entities.notes: Registered Provider Organizations (~250+). Authorized to advise on CMMC but cannot assess. Natural delivery layer.

entities.name: RPs
entities.notes: Registered Practitioners (~5,000+). Individual credential; foundational but rarely sufficient alone.

entities.name: Anecdotes
entities.notes: Evidence-graph approach. Strong for complex multi-framework; CMMC not native.

entities.name: Scrut
entities.notes: Aggressive on price. CMMC support announced 2024; DIB-specific depth still building.

entities.name: TrustCloud
entities.notes: Freemium model disrupting the price floor. CMMC fit limited.

entities.name: Apptega
entities.notes: Sold through MSSP partners. Decent CMMC coverage; channel-first GTM.

entities.name: Hyperproof
entities.notes: Stronger NIST/CMMC alignment than the SaaS-first platforms. Workflow-heavy; better fit for orgs already past 0→1.

entities.name: Primes
entities.notes: Lockheed, Northrop, RTX, etc. Flowing CMMC requirements down to subs. The demand-creation layer.

entities.name: NIST
entities.notes: Sets the standards CMMC enforces. Foundational regulatory authority.

entities.name: CISA
entities.notes: DHS cyber arm. Adjacent to CMMC; increasingly relevant for federal supply-chain risk.

entities.name: ServiceNow GRC
entities.notes: Enterprise GRC heavyweight. Powerful workflow engine, heavy implementation lift. Not targeted at SMBs.

entities.name: Archer
entities.notes: RSA Archer; legacy GRC. Strong risk management, slower pace of innovation.

entities.name: MetricStream
entities.notes: Long-standing enterprise IRM. Broad coverage; not federal/DIB-differentiated.

entities.name: OneTrust
entities.notes: Came from privacy; expanded into GRC. Strong data mapping. CMMC not a core lane.

entities.name: LogicGate
entities.notes: Modern IRM; more flexible than Archer. Enterprise-priced.

entities.name: AuditBoard
entities.notes: Audit-led platform expanding into GRC. SOX origins; CMMC not a stated focus.

entities.name: Workiva
entities.notes: Financial reporting / SOX heavyweight. CMMC not in stated scope.

entities.name: Diligent
entities.notes: Galvanize / HighBond acquisition. Strong board-governance angle.

entities.name: IBM OpenPages
entities.notes: Legacy enterprise GRC. Strong in banking; limited DIB footprint.

entities.name: SAP GRC
entities.notes: Bolted onto SAP installs. DIB SMB is not a stated segment.

entities.name: FutureFeed
entities.notes: CMMC-native tool. Strong on 110-control mapping; lighter on broader GRC and continuous monitoring.

entities.name: ComplianceForge
entities.notes: Policy templates (Secure Controls Framework). Document-first, not SaaS. Heavily used by RPOs.

entities.name: Totem
entities.notes: DIB-focused, SMB-friendly. Strong template library; lighter on continuous monitoring.

entities.name: Cyturus
entities.notes: Adaptive Risk Model. Stronger on quantification than peers.

entities.name: Exostar
entities.notes: DIB identity + supply-chain federation. Mandated by some primes; quasi-infrastructure.

entities.name: Subs
entities.notes: Tier 2–N subcontractors. Where the 0→1 readiness gap is most acute.

entities.name: GAO
entities.notes: Government Accountability Office. Issues critical reports on DoD cybersecurity progress.

entities.name: ISACA
entities.notes: Professional standards body. Frameworks and credentials feed the practitioner layer.

entities.name: Eagle Ridge
entities.notes: Agent-native 0→1 CMMC readiness for DIB SMBs: SSP draft + SPRS submission; POA&M-to-Conditional path via retainer. Operates in the space between authority frameworks on the left, automation platforms on the right, Big 4 above, and Tier 2 C3PAO/RPOs adjacent. Independent of C3PAO conflict rules. Prepares clients to be assessed by Cherry Bekaert, Aprio, RSM, Baker Tilly, and others. Field-tested on the Nereid Biomaterials engagement at proof-of-concept pricing12.

entities.name: Virtru
entities.notes: Email/data encryption with strong DIB footprint.

entities.name: PreVeil
entities.notes: End-to-end encrypted email/files for CUI. Strong DIB SMB fit; lighter lift than GCC High.

entities.name: GCC High
entities.notes: Microsoft Government Community Cloud High. De facto requirement for many DIB contractors handling CUI.

entities.name: Proofpoint
entities.notes: Email security incumbent. CMMC-adjacent via DLP and email controls.

entities.name: Duo Security
entities.notes: Cisco-owned MFA. Core control for the AC/IA family. Practical CMMC building block.

entities.name: DHS
entities.notes: Homeland Security cyber demand. Adjacent ecosystem; future CMMC-like requirements possible.

entities.name: BitSight
entities.notes: Security-ratings leader. Used by primes to assess subs; outside-in only.

entities.name: SecurityScorecard
entities.notes: Direct BitSight competitor. Same outside-in model; expanding into questionnaire automation.

entities.name: UpGuard
entities.notes: Combines TPRM with attack-surface management. Mid-market friendly.

entities.name: Panorays
entities.notes: Questionnaire automation for TPRM. Workflow-heavy.

entities.name: Prevalent
entities.notes: Program-led TPRM. Enterprise-focused.

entities.name: GSA
entities.notes: General Services Administration. Civilian contracting hub; FedRAMP authority.

entities.name: TrustArc
entities.notes: Privacy-program-management heritage. Adjacent to but outside the CMMC stack.

entities.name: Securiti
entities.notes: Privacy + data-security convergence. Enterprise-focused.

entities.name: BigID
entities.notes: Data discovery and classification. CMMC-adjacent via CUI identification.

entities.name: DataGrail
entities.notes: Subject-rights automation. Privacy-focused; CMMC not in stated scope.

entities.name: Osano
entities.notes: Mid-market privacy. Affordable but narrow.

entities.name: SBIR/STTR
entities.notes: Small Business Innovation Research / STTR. Funding source for DIB-adjacent innovation.

entities.name: Deloitte
entities.notes: Big 4 federal practice. Active in CMMC at the prime level; SMB DIB is not the served segment.

entities.name: KPMG
entities.notes: Big 4. Federal compliance practice; targets large enterprise rather than DIB SMB.

entities.name: PwC
entities.notes: Big 4. Enterprise-focused; DIB SMB readiness is not a stated lane.

entities.name: EY
entities.notes: Big 4. Same shape as peers. Top-of-market focus.

entities.name: Accenture Federal
entities.notes: Largest federal systems integrator. Deep DoD relationships; DIB SMB is not the served segment.

entities.name: Booz Allen
entities.notes: Defense-native consulting. Also a C3PAO. Stated focus is CMMC strategy work at scale, not SMB readiness delivery.

entities.name: Leidos
entities.notes: Federal systems integrator. Builds and operates federal systems; SMB advisory not a stated lane.

entities.name: SAIC
entities.notes: Federal services. Same shape as Leidos.

entities.name: CACI
entities.notes: Federal services with strong IC presence.

entities.name: Guidehouse
entities.notes: Federal advisory, spun out of PwC. Compliance-adjacent.

entities.name: ManTech
entities.notes: Federal services. Cyber capabilities; not the SMB advisory layer.

entities.name: GDIT
entities.notes: General Dynamics IT. Federal services prime.

entities.name: Cherry Bekaert
entities.fw: NIST 800-171, CMMC L1/L2, SOC 2, NIST CSF
entities.notes: Top 25 US firm. C3PAO since Dec 2022, reauthorized Jan 20252. Aug 2025 Lifeline Data Centers alliance markets ‘CMMC Fasttrack Implementation’ for SMBs3. An explicit move down-market. Among the most structurally complete competitors: C3PAO + RPO + GovCon client base + SMB product motion. Independence rules prevent them from consulting on engagements they assess.

entities.name: RSM US
entities.notes: Top 5 US accounting firm. Authorized C3PAO7. Secureframe partner enabling streamlined assessments. Broad risk advisory practice with global reach; CMMC is one of many compliance offerings.

entities.name: Forvis Mazars
entities.notes: Top 10 US firm formed by 2024 FORVIS-Mazars merger. Authorized C3PAO7. International reach via Mazars network.

entities.name: HORNE LLP
entities.notes: Mid-sized US firm. CMMC offered as a component of government services and risk advisory practice7. Less marketing surface than peers but in the C3PAO pool.

entities.name: Aprio
entities.fw: CMMC, FedRAMP, SOC, ISO, HITRUST, PCI
entities.notes: Top 25 US firm. C3PAO + FedRAMP 3PAO since June 20254. One of only ~12 firms with both designations. Markets as ‘audit partner not just assessor.’ Strong PNW + tech sector presence. Built proprietary CMMC Accelerator for gap analysis.

entities.name: Baker Tilly
entities.notes: Top 10 US firm. C3PAO since April 2021 via Baker Tilly Data Systems (now Baker Tilly Beers & Cutler, LLC)5. Matt Gilbert (CMMC Leader) was one of the first 100 provisional assessors. 800+ self-reported GovCon clients. Likely the largest installed base in this tier.

entities.name: Schneider Downs
entities.notes: Top 60 US CPA firm. C3PAO since Feb 20256. Among the first 54 nationwide authorizations. Regional focus on PA/OH/WV/NY/MD/DC. CCPs and CCAs in-house.

entities.name: SC&H Group
entities.notes: Mid-market advisory firm; 450+ employees, MD-HQ. Government Contracting is a named industry vertical. Cyber sits inside Risk practice but no C3PAO designation is visible in public materials11. Surfaces CMMC adjacent to existing client relationships rather than as a lead offering. Likely RPO-track.

entities.name: Summit 7
entities.notes: Microsoft-aligned CMMC specialist. Strong GCC High implementation footprint.

entities.name: CyberSheath
entities.notes: DIB-native consulting + managed services. One of the more visible CMMC RPOs.

entities.name: NeoSystems
entities.notes: DIB SMB advisory with GovCon ERP heritage (Deltek). Strong vertical fit.

entities.name: Redspin
entities.notes: Among the first authorized C3PAOs (June 2021). Bridges consulting + assessment.

entities.name: Etactics
entities.notes: Mixed healthcare/DIB compliance. Generalist boutique.

entities.name: Coalfire
entities.notes: Larger specialty firm; strong FedRAMP/3PAO presence. Coalfire Federal authorized as C3PAO Jan 2025. Above the SMB price point.

entities.name: Schellman
entities.notes: Audit firm with strong assurance practice. C3PAO. Mid-large oriented rather than SMB.

entities.name: Arctic Wolf
entities.notes: Concierge MDR. Supports detection controls; not GRC-native.

entities.name: CrowdStrike
entities.notes: EDR/XDR leader. Controls-layer foundational, not GRC delivery.

entities.name: Trustwave
entities.notes: Long-running MSSP. Diversified; CMMC not a stated lead.

entities.name: Rapid7
entities.notes: Vulnerability management + MDR. Adjacent to CMMC controls layer.

entities.name: Optiv
entities.notes: Mega-reseller + services. Channel for many GRC products; mixed advisory layer.
